skill.name: hubspot-integration
skill.description: Guidance for HubSpot CRM integrations covering OAuth, private app tokens, CRM objects, associations, batch operations, webhooks, and custom objects.
skill.summary: Plan and review HubSpot CRM integrations with focused API pattern guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The cited line only describes HubSpot OAuth and CRM API integration topics, with no cryptographic algorithm, executable code, secrets, or prompt injection content found.

content.value_statement: HubSpot integrations require careful handling of authentication, CRM records, associations, and webhook behavior. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for planning and reviewing HubSpot API work.
actual_capabilities: Explains HubSpot OAuth and private app token authentication patterns.
Guides CRM object create, read, update, and delete workflows.
Highlights associations, batch operations, webhooks, and custom objects.
Identifies anti-patterns such as deprecated API keys and excessive individual requests.
Supports implementation planning for Node.js and Python SDK usage.
limitations: Does not include executable integration code or SDK wrappers.
Does not verify live HubSpot account settings or app permissions.
Does not replace current HubSpot API documentation for endpoint details.
Provides brief pattern guidance rather than complete architecture templates.

use_cases.title: Plan CRM Sync Workflows
use_cases.description: Map contacts, companies, deals, and associations before building a HubSpot synchronization service.

use_cases.title: Review Authentication Choices
use_cases.description: Compare OAuth and private app token approaches for public apps and single-account integrations.

use_cases.title: Improve API Efficiency
use_cases.description: Replace repeated single-record calls with batch operations and webhook-driven updates where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between HubSpot OAuth and a private app token for this integration. Ask for missing constraints first.

prompt_templates.title: Design Object Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a HubSpot CRM workflow for creating and updating contacts, companies, and deals with correct association handling.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize API Calls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this HubSpot integration plan and identify where batch operations or webhooks should replace individual requests or polling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of performance and reliability

prompt_templates.title: Audit Integration Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this HubSpot architecture for authentication, object lifecycle handling, webhook processing, custom objects, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: I need to sync contacts from my product into HubSpot.
output_examples.output: A recommended flow covering authentication choice, contact property mapping, duplicate handling, batch write usage, and webhook-based change tracking.

output_examples.input: My integration makes one API call for every deal update.
output_examples.output: A review that flags the individual request pattern and recommends batch operations where HubSpot endpoint support allows it.

output_examples.input: I am building a public HubSpot app.
output_examples.output: Guidance that prioritizes OAuth, token lifecycle handling, permission scopes, and clear separation from private app token patterns.

best_practices: Use OAuth for public HubSpot apps and private app tokens only for controlled single-account integrations.
Model CRM object associations before writing synchronization logic.
Prefer batch operations and webhooks when they fit the integration workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not use deprecated HubSpot API keys for new integrations.
Do not issue individual requests when batch endpoints can handle the work.
Do not poll frequently when webhooks can provide event-driven updates.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to HubSpot directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for integration design and implementation, but it does not execute API calls.

faq.question: Which programming languages does it cover?
faq.answer: The skill mentions Node.js and Python SDK patterns for HubSpot integrations.

faq.question: Can it help with custom objects?
faq.answer: Yes. Custom objects are included as one of the HubSpot integration topics.

faq.question: Does it explain OAuth?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers OAuth as the recommended authentication pattern for public HubSpot apps.

faq.question: Can it reduce HubSpot API usage?
faq.answer: It can guide use of batch operations and webhooks, which may reduce unnecessary calls.

faq.question: Is this a complete HubSpot SDK?
faq.answer: No. It is a concise guidance skill, not a library or a generated SDK.
